rows.notes: Top-centre page title. Handwriting is clear and fully legible; no monetary values present.
Section heading immediately under title; text is clear and there are no amounts on this heading row.
Clear three-column numeric alignment. Pounds '172' and shillings '7' are unambiguous; pence reads '11½' (halfpenny symbol looks like '½').
Pounds column clearly '255'. No shillings or pence written in the corresponding columns (blank). Vertical ruling alignment used to confirm pounds placement.
Single clear numeral '15' under pounds column; shillings and pence columns blank.
Aligned as '65' (£), '19' (s) and pence reads '1½' (halfpenny loop visible). Column boundaries confirmed by neighboring rows.
Clear alignment and digits '19', '7', '2' in the three columns; all within valid ranges.
Digits '28', '6', '10' are crisp and well-aligned with the three vertical ruling columns; currency ranges valid.
Pounds '35' and shillings '1' clear; pence appears as '11½' (halfpenny loop visible). Slight ink flourish on fraction but placement and form consistent with other '½' occurrences.
Entry marked 'Do. Prickett' (abbreviation for 'Ditto') with pounds '14', shillings '2', pence '5½' (halfpenny symbol present). Alignment with column lines used to confirm '5' in pence column.
Pounds '29' and shillings '6' clear; pence reads '1' with no fraction. Handwriting legible and aligned with ruling lines.
Abbreviated 'Do' then 'Beaton six years'; amounts read as '8' (pounds), '10' (shillings), '1' (pence). Clear alignment; no fraction present.
Values '12', '19', and pence '11½' are clearly written; halfpenny symbol consistent with others on page.

Pounds '11' and shillings '2' are clear. Pence area shows a very small fractional mark (looks like a quarter/pent mark '¼') with little or no whole pence digit; interpreted as 0 pence and a farthing (0.25). Slight ink faintness makes the absence of a whole-pence digit somewhat uncertain.

Pounds '4' and shillings '5' clear. Pence reads '1' with a following three-quarter symbol (looks like '3q' or a small '¾'); interpreted as 1 3/4d (pence whole 1, fraction 0.75). Fraction symbol is slightly cramped but consistent with three‑quarter form on merchant ledgers.
Subsection heading under main list; handwriting clear and no amounts on this heading row.
Pounds '22', shillings '5', pence '2½' (halfpenny visible) — digits and fraction clear and aligned with ruling lines.

Pounds '10' and shillings '18' clear. Pence reads as '9½' — halfpenny symbol visible but a little cramped, producing minor ambiguity between 9½ and 8½; chosen 9½ based on vertical alignment with other pence entries.
Clear '10' (pounds), '5' (shillings) and pence '11½' (halfpenny symbol). Alignment consistent with column lines.
Pounds '17' and shillings '1' clear. Pence column shows a dash/long mark indicating zero pence rather than a numeric value; interpreted as 0d.
Pounds '2' and shillings '18' clear. Pence reads '6¼' — small quarter-farthings mark visible after the '6'. The quarter symbol is slightly faint but shape and position support 0.25 interpretation.
